_schema_notes.coverage: Authoritative schema for all DID formats — DD Form 1664 (pre-2014) and free-text (post-2014 MIL-STD-963C)
_schema_notes.did_format: Set to 'dd_form_1664' or 'free_text'. Determines which fields are expected to be populated.
_schema_notes.963c_compliant: True if the DID was prepared under MIL-STD-963C (2014 or later). False for older DIDs.
_schema_notes.reserved_fields: Fields marked RESERVED are present in one format but not the other. Always include them — set to empty string or empty array when not applicable.
_schema_notes.preparation_instructions: Official MIL-STD-963C name for what was previously called 'requirements'. Contains the numbered sections of the DID. All section numbers are normalized to strictly numeric dot-notation (1, 1.1, 1.1.1). Letter-based source numbering (2.a, 3.1.b) is converted to numeric equivalents (2.1, 3.1.2).
_schema_notes.asterisk: True if the requirement number is followed by * in the source document (conditional requirement).

title: Technical Data Package Review Report
summary: This Data Item Description outlines the format and content for a Technical Data Package Review Report, identifying deficiencies and improvements needed before production or release, and supporting Engineering Change Proposals.
description_purpose: The Technical Data Package Review Report specifies deficiencies or improvements in technical data packages that must be corrected prior to start of production or prior to release of data for other authorized use. The report shall serve as a basis for monitoring the submission of Engineering Change Proposals (ECP).
application_interrelationship: This Data Item Description (DID) contains the format and content preparation instructions for the data product generated by the specific and discrete task requirement as delineated in the contract. Submission of this report does not waive the requirement for the submission of an ECP. This DID supersedes DI-E-1127.
distribution_statement: DISTRIBUTION STATEMENT A: Approved for public release; distribution is unlimited.

children.title: Format.
children.content: The Technical Data Package Review Report format shall be contractor selected. Unless effective presentation would be degraded, the initially used format arrangement shall be used for all subsequent submissions.

children.title: Content.
children.content: The Technical Data Package Review Report content shall certify as to the following:

children.title: Adequacy and accuracy of drawing dimensions, fits and tolerances.
Adequacy and accuracy of all mechanical and electrical circuitry.
Adequacy of test procedures to assure conformity with requirements.
Overall completeness and adequacy of all documentation for manufacturing purposes.
Adequacy and accuracy of supplementary quality assurance provisions.
Error listing.
children.content: The report shall list the documentation found to be in error and unsatisfactory for the production and test of the item. When errors are found in the documentation, the report shall list the document by nonmenclature (e.g., number, title, revision level, date), provide a description of the error and specify the reason for the proposed change. When extensive changes are applicable to a drawing/document, a marked up copy of the drawing/document shall be attached.

children.title: Contractor statement.
children.content: The report shall include a statement that the contractor has ascertained that all of the Technical Data Package documentation, with the proposed changes, are adequate for production.
